key_insight: The paper decomposes unbounded PDE domains into near-field and far-field regions, using separate randomized neural subnetworks and linear least-squares fitting to handle local structure and exterior decay efficiently.
engineering_relevance: This offers a practical surrogate solver for large-scale exterior-domain PDEs where truncation boundaries, irregular geometries, or mixed near/far behavior make standard methods costly or inaccurate.
method: Assign different randomized subnetworks to different spatial regimes (near-field vs. far-field).
Enforce coupling through boundary and interface conditions, then solve only output coefficients by linear least squares.
Use Petrov–Galerkin for semi-unbounded elliptic problems and collocation for fully unbounded, perforated, and time-dependent cases.
evidence_signal: The abstract reports numerical experiments on Poisson and time-dependent Schrödinger equations, along with a proven approximation result and an error decomposition covering approximation, quadrature/consistency, and optimization effects.
watch_item: Performance may depend on how well the domain split, quadrature, and interface constraints are chosen, since the method’s guarantees are conditional and least-squares accuracy can be sensitive to setup.
paper_title: Domain-Decomposed Randomized Neural Networks for Partial Differential Equations in Unbounded Domains
